reasoning_summary: For live run 20260421_114147_bf16_gemm_v1_4784c8d at 46.509056 ms, restoring the old historical PTX anchor did not recover the pre-workload-change 24 ms regime, which means the current loop must stop treating that old accepted base as the active recovery target. Under the latest workload, the last two PTX runs cluster around 46.37-46.51 ms, with 20260421_113859_bf16_gemm_v1_6668d21 at 46.366718 ms slightly ahead via 198 regs/thread but with worse barrier. So round 5 should first re-anchor on the best current-workload surface, then attack barrier on top of that local winner instead of continuing to restore toward a stale baseline.
